title: mallotus insularum
intro: Mallotus insularum, also known as the insular mallotus, is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands, specifically the Line Islands. It has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine, where its roots and bark are valued for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, and is often used to treat colds and flu. Mallotus insularum is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, where the roots and bark are steeped in water to make a medicinal tea.
